figures.caption: Figure 1: Overview of the simple-signal basis and functional-coefficient fitting.

Figure 3: Residual dependence diagnostics based on the Ljung–Box test. In panel (a), each dot represents the p-value of the test for a single session, and red dots denote sessions with p-values less than 0.05.
Figure 2: Response time (RT) distributions by subject and session.

Figure 4: Average functional connectivity across all trials, measured by Pearson correlation between pairs of EEG channels over time. The heatmap indicates substantial dependence among EEG channels, especially for nearby locations. Channel labels are color coded by estimated effect type: null, simple, or complex.
Figure 5: Visualization of the raw EEG signal and the main preprocessing steps.
Table 1: Mean and p5%, 95%q quantile of the relative excess risk (RER) computed over 200 simulation replicates under three scenarios.
Table 2: Variable-selection and form-identification metrics over 200 simulation replicates under three scenarios.
